name: Risk Reward Room
description: An optional room or encounter that offers stronger rewards in exchange for extra danger, resource loss, curses, elite enemies, or route commitment.
design_notes: Risk-reward rooms need honest previews and enforceable outcomes. The player should know the type of danger they are accepting, but not necessarily every hidden detail. The implementation should connect room_node_id, encounter_id, reward_pool_id, curse_modifier_id, and completion state so the room cannot be farmed, skipped after taking rewards, or saved in a contradictory state.
design_purpose: Let players voluntarily increase run pressure for better rewards.
Create meaningful route decisions on generated floors.
Give advanced players a way to convert skill, confidence, or resources into build power.
player_fantasy: The player feels tempted to bet the run on a dangerous room because the payoff could define the build.

parameters.name: room_node_id
parameters.description: Generated room node that owns accepted and completed state.

parameters.name: encounter_id
parameters.description: Encounter selected after the risk is accepted.

parameters.name: reward_pool_id
parameters.description: Reward pool granted after completion or partial success.

parameters.name: risk_preview_id
parameters.description: Player-facing summary of what is at stake.

parameters.name: elite_modifier_id
parameters.description: Optional modifier applied to enemies or room hazards.

parameters.name: entry_commitment_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule that controls leaving, reward access, and failure state.

parameters.name: completion_state_id
parameters.description: Persisted room state used by map, rewards, and saves.

edge_cases: The player accepts the risk, leaves the room, and reloads before completion or failure is resolved.
Reward pickup becomes reachable before the encounter actually clears.
A curse or payment is applied, but the room generation fails and no encounter starts.
A daily run needs the same risk preview and reward result for every player.
The room appears on a route where low-health players cannot reasonably avoid it.
common_bugs: Room state resets on reload and lets players retry the risk after seeing the encounter.
Rewards are generated from a fresh seed after completion and differ from the preview or replay.
Exit doors unlock from enemy despawn instead of authoritative encounter clear state.
Elite modifiers stack with floor modifiers in an undocumented order and create unfair combinations.
Failure state removes the reward but leaves the curse or payment unapplied.
balancing_notes: The best risk rooms offer a real refusal path; mandatory risk feels like baseline difficulty rather than a choice.
Rewards should match the danger type: resource payment can grant build power, while combat risk can grant rare items or route advantage.
Preview clarity changes fairness; hidden details work better when the category of risk is still readable.
accessibility_notes: Make the accepted risk readable in text before the player commits.
Provide enough contrast, audio alternatives, and map labels for optional high-danger rooms.
Consider anti-frustration grace rules if accidental entry would otherwise ruin long runs.

implementation_notes.unity: Store room_node_id, completion_state_id, encounter_id, and reward seed in run state before opening the room. Commit payment or curse acceptance and lock doors through one transaction, then grant reward_pool_id only after the encounter director reports a clear result.
implementation_notes.godot: Use room metadata Resources for risk previews and encounter bindings. Persist accepted state before scene transitions, and route enemy deaths, trap completion, and reward spawning through a single room state node.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement risk rooms as level instances with a replicated RoomStateComponent for networked runs. Server authority should validate entry commitment, encounter clear, reward grants, and exit unlocks to prevent reward farming.
implementation_notes.web: Generate risk offers from deterministic room graph data and persist completion_state_id in the run record. Phaser or custom maps should render the warning from risk_preview_id and never derive reward eligibility from visual chest state alone.

relationships.reason: Risk-reward rooms need floor graph placement, route visibility, and reward node metadata.
Risk rooms can offer stronger reward rolls after the player accepts extra danger or constraints.
Health pressure makes optional danger meaningful because entering a risk room can reduce later run safety.

example_games.title: Slay the Spire
example_games.note: Uses elite fights and events that trade danger for stronger rewards.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Uses optional doors and challenge encounters that can improve run rewards.
